entry.title: Demystifying Peppol’s Message Types: Transport Acknowledgement, MLR, BLR
entry.content:
Are you exchanging electronic documents with your business partners through the Peppol network using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition? If so, you have probably come across terms such as Transport Acknowledgement (Acks), Message Level Response (MLR) or Business Level Response (BLR). And you might already know that all three refer to types of messages that are exchanged in Peppol's 4-corner model. However, it might not be completely clear to you when and why each of these message types is sent, what information they convey, or how they look in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app of your SAP S/4HANA, SAP ERP, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. With this blog, we would like to bring some clarity into these questions.

Additionally, we will talk about message types developed by SAP to update the status of the electronic document in the SAP business system. Even though, these message types are not part of the OpenPeppol specification, we are mentioning them for the sake of completeness of all message types in the process of sending an electronic document using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

 

Message Types

In general, you can think of the message types as a multi-stage check to ensure the reliable transmission of an electronic document over the Peppol network. Each message type confirms the status of a different stage in the transmission process.

Let's walk through the different message types using a concrete example. In the example, your business system is SAP S/4HANA Cloud, so the sender (corner 1) is represented by the Manage Electronic Documents app, and you are using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition (corner 2) to send electronic documents through the Peppol network to your business partners.

Example: Message types exchanged in Peppol’s 4-corner model when you send electronic documents

The starting point in the Peppol 4-corner model is the first corner where you, as the sender, submit an invoice in the Manage Electronic Documents app to your business partner. The document is then sent to the sender access point (corner 2) which is your SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition - this is the corner where the first message type is created.

Message type: Confirmation from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition

Message Type: Confirmation from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
This message type is not part of the message types defined by OpenPeppol but was developed by SAP as an additional layer to ensure that your document has securely and correctly reached the sender access point.

In the cloud edition (your sender access point), two things are checked:

1. Has the document arrived at the sender access point?

  • If yes, the cloud edition sends a message to your business system and the status in your Manage Electronic Documents app changes to Received by Sender Access Point.
  • If no, the cloud edition does not send a message to your business system and the status in your Manage Electronic Documents app stays in Sending Requested.

2. Does the document pass the validation checks? The validation checks involve comparing the document with the Schematron rules stored in the sender access point and making sure the receiving participant in the Peppol network can be identified by the sender access point. If the document doesn’t pass the validation checks, the cloud edition sends a message to your business system and the status in the app changes to Error at Sender Access Point.

To resolve errors that appear at this stage you can check the Troubleshooting guide or the SAP Help Portal.

Message type: Transport Acknowledgement (Acks)

Message type: Transport Acknowledgement (Acks)

When your document has been sent to the sender access point and has successfully passed validation there, the sender access point (corner 2) forwards the document to the receiver access point (corner 3). The receiver access point creates the next message type – the transport acknowledgement.

The forwarding of the document to the receiver access point as well as the transport acknowledgement is accomplished using the Applicability Statement 4 (AS4) messaging protocol. It is based on web services standards and is commonly used for the secure and reliable exchange of documents such as purchase orders, invoices, and shipping notices.

The message transfer in AS4 involves the following steps:

  1. Message Creation: The sender access point creates the message to be sent, which typically includes the actual document and any necessary metadata.
  2. Message Packaging: The message is then packaged according to the AS4 standard, which defines the structure and format of the message. The packaging includes adding headers, security information, and other necessary elements.
  3. Adding Digital Signature: After the message has been packaged, a digital signature is generated using the sender's private key. This digital signature is added to the message. This not only validates the sender’s identity, but also ensures the integrity of the message during transmission as it can be used to detect any changes made to the message after it has been signed.
  4. Message Transmission: The packaged message along with the digital signature is transmitted over the Peppol network, using HTTP protocols.
  5. Message Receipt: Once the receiver access point receives the message, it verifies the digital signature using the sender’s public key. If the verification is successful, it indicates that the message was not tampered with during transmission and was indeed sent by the claimed sender.
  6. Transport Acknowledgment: After the successful verification of the digital signature, the receiver access point sends back an acknowledgment to the sender access point to confirm the successful receipt of the message.

Once the transport acknowledgement reaches the cloud edition (corner 2), the cloud edition sends a message to your business system (corner 1) that triggers the status update in the Manage Electronic Documents app to Acknowledged by Receiver Access Point.

In the case of an unsuccessful message transfer using the AS4 protocol, such as a header error in the message, the receiver access point can’t send a transport acknowledgment to the sender access point. However, it's important to note that the cloud edition (corner 2) still ensures transparency in this scenario by sending a message to your business system, triggering a status change in the Manage Electronic Documents app to Rejected by Receiver Access Point.

Overall, AS4 ensures secure and reliable message transfer in B2B communication, and the use of digital signatures adds an additional layer of security and trust to the process.

Message type: Message Level Response (MLR)

Message type: Message Level Response (MLR)

The receiver access point performs another round of validations on the invoice, particularly focusing on the Schematron rules. Since there are multiple versions of these rules, the sender access point and the receiver access point might be checking against different versions. Hence, a document might pass the validation at the sender access point and fail at the receiver access point.

The message that can be sent by the receiver access point to inform about the validation status is referred to as Message Level Response (MLR). Let’s have a look at the scenarios:

  • You have enabled receiving MLR in the Configure Participants app of the cloud edition.
    • If the receiver access point supports the sending of MLR, you receive an MLR message with the information whether the receiver access point accepted or rejected the electronic document after the validation. In your Manage Electronic Documents app you’ll therefore see either the status Accepted by Rec. Acc. Point or Valid. Error at Rec. Acc Point.
    • If the receiver access point doesn’t support the sending of MLR, you won’t receive an MLR message. In your Manage Electronic Documents app you’ll therefore see the status Acknowl. by Rec. Acc. Point.
  • You haven’t enabled receiving MLR in the Configure Participants app of the cloud edition: Then of course you won’t receive an MLR message and in your Manage Electronic Documents app you’ll see the status Acknowl. by Rec. Acc. Point.

MLR messages contribute to the overall robustness, reliability, and traceability of the Peppol network by enabling businesses to ensure that their business messages have been successfully received and processed, and quickly identify and rectify issues.

For more information about the Message Level Response, check out this page in the help portal: Message Level Response

Message type: Business Level Response (BLR)

Message type: Business Level Response (BLR)

Finally, the receiver access point forwards the document to the receiver (corner 4). The receiver system, or the users themselves, can then decide whether they wish to communicate something to the sender. This means, BLR messages are optional and may not be sent by the receiver. This message, originating from the receiver, is called a Business Level Response (BLR), as it provides information on the business decision relating to the business document.

Unlike the MLR, which is an automated technical confirmation of the message receipt and processing, the BLR concerns the actual outcome of the business message.

The BLR message is forwarded through all the corners and depending on the BLR the statuses in the Manage Electronic Documents app can change. For example, if the original document is an invoice, the following statuses can appear:

  • In Process by Receiver
  • Under Query by Receiver
  • Conditionally Accepted by Rec.
  • Acknowledged by Receiver
  • Accepted by Receiver
  • Rejected by Receiver
  • Partially Paid by Receiver
  • Paid by Receiver

For more information about the Business Level Response, check out this page in the help portal: Business Level Response.

How to check all those messages in your system

You can trace all the messages from the beginning to the end of the exchange process in the Manage Electronic Documents app in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system or in the eDocument Cockpit in your SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA system.

In the Manage Electronic Documents app, expand the Details of the electronic document.

Screenshot of the Manage Electronic Documents app with focus on the Details button

In the Attachments section, you can choose Preview to view the messages.

Screenshot of the Manage Electronic Documents app with focus on the Attachments section and the Preview button

In the eDocument Cockpit, navigate to Goto > History, to access the History of the electronic document.

Screenshot of the eDocument Cockpit with focus on the History button

In the EDocument History view, you can scroll to the right until you see the File Name column. By double-clicking on the files you can view the messages.

Screenshot of the EDocument History view in the eDocument Cockpit with the focus on the File Name column

How to enable the message types

If you are in the latest support package for your release, all message types that we previously described are already enabled in your business system for all countries supported by SAP that exchange invoices over the Peppol network. For the sending of Business Level Response, we currently only support Invoice Responses with Accepted and Rejected status, with the exception of Singapore, where we also support Invoice Responses with Acknowledged status.

In the cloud edition however, you must activate the Message Types in the Configure Participants app. This can be done while setting up a participant for your company, by selecting the various message types under the Additional Document Types field. See Registering Your Company to Send Electronic Documents in the Peppol Network.

If you are not in the latest support package of your SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP system, you can manually implement the following SAP Note: 2783795 - Electronic Document Processing for Peppol Countries/Regions: Implementation Overview.

Further information

For further information about the message types that are part of the OpenPeppol specification, check this page: BIS Message Level Response 3.0

For detailed information about the subcategory Invoice Response of the Business Level Response type, check this page: BIS Invoice Response 3.2

Give us Feedback!

Please share your feedback and thoughts in a comment. If you have questions about SAP Document Compliance, you can post your question here.

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence & Technology in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Welcome to my blog post on Artificial Intelligence and Technologies, covering the latest features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2408. In this release, we are excited to introduce a range of AI-driven functionalities that enhance user experience, improve operational efficiency, and support better decision-making. With features like Joule, AI-assisted financial insights, and intelligent resource matching, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release 2408 is set to enhance your business operations, making them more intuitive, accurate, and efficient. Kudos to all our developers for their hard work and dedication.

 Watch my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

 

 

 

Joule

Joule is the new AI copilot in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, revolutionizing the way users interact with the system. It acts as your companion in navigating the complex SAP Ecosystem, offering transparent, role-based access to relevant processes from any location. With Joule, you experience a unified and delightful user interface across SAP's solution portfolio.

Joule facilitates conversational interactions, enabling seamless communication between humans and computers. It supports three primary conversational patterns:

  1. Navigational: Assisting users in locating the functionality they need within SAP systems.
  2. Transactional: Helping users efficiently complete various tasks.
  3. Informational: Retrieving relevant information from existing documents.

Leveraging generative AI, Joule provides many benefits, including:

  • Easy Navigation: Streamlined access to applications.
  • Fast Insights: Quick retrieval of business object information, such as purchase orders.
  • Seamless Access to Content: Summarized user enablement content for efficient task execution.

Moreover, Joule operates within various SAP BTP environments, including Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Kubernetes, or others. It is a multitenant application, ensuring data isolation and independent provisioning for different consumers.

Joule’s capabilities will be available in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in Continuous Delivery this year, offering users a more intuitive and productive experience.

 

 

AI-Assisted Financial Business Insights in Finance (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

The "Virtual Analyst" within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition transforms financial data analysis by providing immediate explanations and summarizations of insights in natural language. This AI feature allows users to quickly discover what’s meaningful within selected data in Review Booklets, analyzing extensive data sets, highlighting dependencies, and proposing actions. Benefits include:

  • Immediate Insights: Quick explanations and summarizations of data insights in natural language.
  • Comprehensive Analysis: Analyzes extensive data sets, highlights dependencies, and proposes actions.
  • Intuitive Visualizations: Presents findings in visual, tabular, and plain language formats, aiding management reporting and decision-making.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

 

AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions in Finance (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

This feature leverages AI to automatically extract key information from natural language sentences or Excel files, setting up jurisdiction-based tax configurations with minimal manual input. The AI-assisted configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions reduces the effort required for users to configure tax settings accurately, minimizing manual errors. Benefits include:

  • Accuracy and Efficiency: Reduces manual errors and enhances setup accuracy.
  • Modernized Onboarding: Natural language interactions and an intuitive interface streamline the onboarding process, cutting down both time and costs.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

AI-Assisted Error Resolution (SAP Advanced Financial Closing)

AI-Assisted Error Resolution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers a comprehensive solution to common error situations. It generates understandable explanations of technical error messages, providing end-users with detailed resolution steps. AI-Assisted Error Resolution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers:

  •   Comprehensible Explanations: Generates understandable explanations of technical error messages, making complex issues more accessible to end-users.
  •   Step-by-Step Guides: Provides detailed resolution steps to help users solve errors independently.
  •   Time Savings: Expedites error resolution during critical financial processes, reducing delays and operational pressure. This feature helps end-users resolve common errors independently, improving operational efficiency and reducing reliance on SAP support.

This feature significantly enhances the efficiency and effectiveness of financial reporting and decision-making processes.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

 

AI-assisted Matching of Employees to Projects (SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Projects)

This advanced feature supports resource managers by intelligently matching skilled employees to project needs. It considers similar skills to those requested and maintained in the candidate profiles, displaying both the requested skills and the skills present in the profiles of matching candidates.

  •   Skill Matching: AI identifies candidates with the required or similar skills for project needs, simplifying the search process.
  •   Efficient Search: Resource managers can quickly find suitable employees for projects, ensuring optimal skill allocation and improved project outcomes.

This tool enhances the efficiency of project staffing and ensures that the right skills are allocated to the right projects, optimizing resource management processes.Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link.

 

 

AI-Assisted Smart Summarization (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

With AI-Assisted Smart Summarization, users can obtain concise summaries of object pages based on SAP Fiori elements apps. This feature streamlines decision-making by providing clear and actionable information. Capabilities include:

  • Streamline Decision Making: Obtain concise summaries of object pages based on SAP Fiori elements apps.
  • Language Translation: Translate summaries into various languages.
  • Effortless Sharing: Use summaries as a foundation for follow-ups and share them easily across preferred platforms.

The summarizations can be used as a foundation for follow-ups and shared easily across preferred platforms, enhancing collaboration and communication.

This functionality is available for beta testing without additional licensing costs.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

 

AI-Assisted Easy Filter (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

This feature enhances the filtering experience by generating results for inquiries in SAP Fiori elements-based list report applications using natural language. Users can verify and refine filter values through the classical filter experience or choose whatever filtering method suits their needs best. This feature enhances the filtering experience by:

  • Natural Language Queries: Generates results for inquiries in SAP Fiori elements-based list report applications using natural language.
  • Flexible Filtering: Allows verification and refinement of filter values through classical filter experiences.

The AI-Assisted Easy Filter makes it simpler and faster to find the information users need, improving the efficiency of data retrieval and analysis.

This functionality is available for beta testing without additional licensing costs.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

Auto Enablement (Turnkey) of AI Use Cases

The Auto Enablement (Turnkey) feature simplifies the activation and management of AI use cases within SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It reduces or eliminates the manual effort required to initially activate and manage machine learning (ML) scenarios, ensuring that customers do not need extensive data science or process knowledge. This feature simplifies the activation and management of AI use cases by:

  • Reducing Manual Effort: Automates the initial activation and lifecycle management of ML scenarios.
  • No Expertise Required: Does not require data science or process knowledge from the customer.
  • Enhanced Model Quality: Improves business value by increasing model quality.

Users can easily activate and monitor AI use cases through a simple "Turnkey" switch.

 

 

Gen AI Use Cases Support via ISLM

The ISLM component in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enables seamless integration between applications and large language models (LLMs). It supports out-of-the-box generative AI use cases, facilitating their exploration, deployment, and activation. The ISLM component in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enables:

  • Seamless Integration: Acts as an AI integration component between applications and large language models (LLMs).
  • Out-of-Box Use Cases: Facilitates exploration and management of generative AI-based use cases.
  • Prompt Management: Provides basic prompt management for creating, generating, and extending prompt templates in business contexts.

These features empower users to leverage generative AI effectively within their business processes.

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X. 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Empowering SAP Partners - Service Partner User Management Application
entry.content:
SAP customers rely on high-quality implementation and operation services by SAP partners for their SAP cloud solutions. As an element of these services, SAP partners will often perform support-related activities and will engage with SAP support on behalf of their customers.

To empower these SAP partners, and to enable an expanded customer experience, SAP has introduced the service partner user management application, through which customers can authorize their partner’s S-user IDs to create and manage cases in SAP for Me on their behalf. This application is a key enabler for partner’s support related service quality, optimized troubleshooting, and collaboration between SAP and partners. In addition, it benefits SAP customers by resulting in accelerated troubleshooting of cases, higher transparency about partner behavior, and fewer S-user IDs to manage for partner users. SAP recommends that customers and partners leverage this application for any such scenarios where partners are engaging with SAP support at the request and on behalf of their customers. 

This blog post will share an overview of this application and the process and will be regularly updated when new features are added.

Process Overview

This application is natively hosted in SAP for Me. The process involves the use of the following cards in SAP for Me under the Users & Contacts dashboard:

  • Manage Service Partner Users: for use by customer’s cloud administrator, to invite and disengage partner’s S-user IDs, as well as manage the authorizations, installations, and validity periods
  • Service Partner User Cockpit: for use by partner S-users to accept invitations from customer as well as disengage the authorization

The process is completed as follows in SAP for Me:

1) Customer invites partner S-user(s) using Manage Service Partner Users (can be done one by one, or via CSV file upload)

2) Partner S-user accepts invitation in the Service Partner User Cockpit

3) Customer assigns the authorization to partner S-user, as well as specifying the installations and/or tenants they are authorized for and the authorization validity date

4) Partner S-user is now able to create and manage cases on behalf of customer within the Get Support application using their own S-user ID

New Features:

As of 6/1/2024 -

Customers now have the option to invite multiple S-users at a time using a CSV upload.

Restrictions:

  • CSV file only
  • Up to 200 entries

References

  • Customer Guide: https://d.dam.sap.com/a/sz1zkWg 
  • Customer KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/2518600

entry.title: Post Goods Receipt Without Reference in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Exploring Stock Procurement Processes: Goods Receipts Without Reference in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Discover how the "Post Goods Receipt Without Reference" app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud can streamline your procurement process by allowing you to post goods receipts without a purchasing document. This app is particularly useful for unplanned goods receipts that do not reference a preceding document.

Key Features of the Post Goods Receipt Without Reference App:

  • Post goods receipts without a purchasing document.
  • Enter details such as material number, quantity, unit, plant, storage location, and stock type.
  • Confirm entries and post the goods receipt.
  • Modify posting date and document date if needed.
  • Optionally, add notes and delivery notes.

App: Goods Receipt Without Reference app.

How to Use the App:

  1. Choose the Post Goods Receipt Without Reference app.
  2. Enter the required information:
    • Material number
    • Quantity / Unit
    • Plant
    • Storage Location
    • Stock Type
  3. Confirm your entry and choose Post.

In the General Information section, you can check and, if necessary, change the posting date and document date. You can also optionally enter a note and a delivery note (free text).

Visual Guide:

  • Before Posting:

  • After Posting: 

  • Generated Documentation:
  • Automated Flow in SAP Cloud:

Leverage the Post Goods Receipt Without Reference app to manage your unplanned goods receipts efficiently and maintain accurate stock levels.

entry.title: Where can I learn GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium Implementation and Adoption?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium Implementation and Adoption learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will introduce you to the core features and activities for GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium implementation and adoption.


Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be equipped to drive and implement GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium by applying the knowledge learned about integration between SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Network, buyer account management, transaction rules, and Trading Partner enablement.
 
Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: 使用8D问题解决方法的质量问题解决流程
entry.content:
面对质量缺陷问题，我们有很多工具可以用来分析和解决问题。其中一个比较典型的就是8D问题解决法（8 Discipline Problem Solving），也称团队导向问题解决法。在SAP公有云系统，我们也提供了基于8D问题解决法的问题解决流程。

当您在质量检验过程中发现了质量缺陷，可以在管理缺陷应用中针对某一个缺陷去启动问题解决流程：

这时，系统会自动为您创建一个问题解决流程的号码，点击该号码可以跳转至问题解决流程的详细界面中。

8D模型的问题解决流程，细分为8个工作步骤，包括组建团队、对缺陷问题的描述、根本原因的分析和阐述、后续更正/实施/预防的操作和措施等。

D1团队，目的是组建一个具备工艺和产品知识，具有分析和解决问题能力的小组，一般会有团队成员和团队领导。

点击问题解决流程中的D1团队，右上角的状态：设置为“处理中”，然后就可以添加小组成员了。

在D2问题描述中，可详细说明缺陷问题的情况，包括表象、数据等等。在D3包含操作定义为避免问题扩大或持续恶化而执行的临时遏制措施。

对于该缺陷问题，您需要分析出现问题的原因，并在D4根本原因中记录包括所有可能的潜在原因。在这里可以选择已定义好的原因代码，也可以添加详细的原因描述。

在D5定义的更正操作中您可以针对以上原因分别创建更正任务：

在质量任务中，您选择预定义好的任务代码，详细描写具体任务情况，指定任务处理人，和预计需要的工作时间：

完成定义更正操作中的任务后，系统会针对每一条任务生成一个实施更正操作的后继质量任务：

完成更正操作后，可定义相关的预防措施，如修改现有管理系统设置、更新设计图纸等：

最后在D8祝贺团队中对小组工作进行总结和祝贺。

至此，完成了整个质量问题的解决流程。

————————————————————————————————————————————
**本博文中涉及到的截图均来源于SAP内部测试环境。

entry.title: A Guide to Configure Maximum Proposals for Planned Orders & Fixed lot size of 1 for Production Order
entry.content:
A Guide for Setting Up Maximum Order Proposals for Planned Orders and Creating Production Orders with Fixed Lot Size One

Welcome to our blog!

In high-tech industries like semiconductors, tracking serial and batch numbers of BOM components is vital for effective after-sales service and quality management. One effective strategy to achieve this is by creating production orders with a fixed lot size of one. This blog post provides a comprehensive guide on how to set up and manage these production orders. By following the steps outlined, manufacturing teams can ensure accurate tracking of serial numbers and sub-level BOM components, ultimately enhancing overall production efficiency and traceability.

Business Requirement:

In high-tech industries like semiconductors, the manufacturing team aims to monitor the serial numbers of finished products and the sub-level BOM components used in their production. To achieve this, they maintain production orders with a fixed lot size of one and set the maximum order proposal to the highest possible limit (as the default limit for planned order proposals is 50, which might cause errors if the requirement quantity exceeds 50 at the time of MRP run). This method allows for precise traceability of lower-level BOM components and tracking of the serial numbers or batch numbers of components consumed in the manufacturing of the final finished product.

Below is a step-by-step process on how to set-up maximum order proposal for planned orders and to create production orders of fixed lot size ‘1’.

  • Maintain the maximum order proposal for Planned orders by accessing the following configurational path ‘Demand and Supply > Supply Chain Planning > Material Planning > Material Requirement Planning (MRP) > Define Plant Parameters Overview.’

         

  • Maintain the Number of planned orders to the maximum limit ‘999’ and save the configuration.

         

  • Maintain fixed lot size of ‘1’ in material master data using Fiori app Change Material (MM02).

         

  • Maintain Independent Requirement for the Material/Plant using Fiori app ‘Maintain PIRs.’ (In the below example, 5 quantities maintained for the period M08.2024).

         

  • Validate the Independent requirement maintained for that material/plant using Fiori app ‘Monitor Material Coverage – Net/Individual segments.

         

  • Perform MRP Run by clicking on the button ‘Start MRP Run’ in Fiori app Monitor Material Coverage.

         

  • Planned orders with fixed lot size ‘1’ has been created based on the requirement and these planned orders can be converted to production orders each with quantity 1EA using Fiori app Monitor material coverage or MD04 (Stock Requirement List).

         

  • Release the Production Order using Fiori app ‘Manage Production Orders’ or CO02 (Change Production Order)

         

  • Complete Goods Issue (Mvt. Type 261) of the BOM components using Fiori app ‘Post Goods Movement’.

         

  • Complete Goods Receipt (Mvt. Type 101) of the final product using Fiori app ‘Post Goods Receipt for Production Orders’.

         

Item Detail Screen:

         

  • Perform Confirmation of production order using Fiori app ‘Confirm Production Operation’.

         

  • Technically complete the production order using Fiori app CO02 (Change Production Order)
  • Once the order is complete, serial numbers issued and received in a production order can be tracked using a custom serial number tracker report. Please refer the SAP Community link to get the details on how to build a serial number tracker report for production materials in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Implementing a robust tracking system for serial numbers and BOM components in the semiconductor industry is crucial for maintaining product integrity and traceability. By following the step-by-step process outlined above, manufacturing teams can effectively set up maximum order proposals for planned orders and create production orders with a fixed lot size of one to a maximum limit of 999 orders. This meticulous approach ensures that every component used in the manufacturing process is accounted for, leading to improved quality control and customer satisfaction.

Hope this is useful and for any further queries, please post your queries in the SAP Community Portal

Happy Learning!!

entry.title: Simplified Transport Management
entry.content:
Are you a small medium size company? Are you a bit overwhelmed with the "flexibility" that the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3-system landscape, brings you? Transport management is quite a different concept to adapt if you are new to the SAP world. For me, who "grow older" every day from my R/3 days, it was quite natural that now in Public Cloud there is this Dev, Test and Production landscape. And you will need to take care of transports, grouping of your configuration setting changes, moving them across, release, export, import, forward, import ... from one environment to another.

But we are in cloud now, you say, shouldn't these be a bit easier? Shouldn't it be automated to a certain extent? You are not alone. SAP hears you as well and you now have an option! 

You can now choose between Simple or Flexible transport mode for your business process configuration transports to suit your project needs. 

SAP, being SAP, LOL, the introduction of this nice little feature is kind of "hidden". This is a setting in the Extensibility Setting app under the Key User Extensibility tab for your Business Process Configuration settings. To use this new “ Simple Mode”, you do so by changing the Flexible Transport mode to “Off”. This is a landscape setting that carries across Dev, Test and Production. You need to have an Extensibility Specialist role assigned in order to see the app in your Development System in order to make a change.

When you choose the “Simple Mode”, you can create or have only One modifiable transport for all Business Process Configuration type (aka Customizing type) of transports in Export Customizing Transports app in the Development System Customizing Tenant. With this setting, once a week import is automated and enforced in your Test System. SAP delivered business content from update and upgrade (aka SAP Scheduled collections) is set to auto-forward to your Production System. 

Despite the awkwardness of selecting the option, it is indeed a nice feature to have in the public cloud ERP space. For example, a project doesn't have multiple country rollouts in scope, have limited business process activations, or with little extension considerations can simplify its overall transport process with Simple Transport Mode. 

You should evaluate and make a decision at the beginning of your Realize phase. You can always adjust that setting when you don’t have any open transports during the project. For example, if you are executing in multiple sprints during the realization phase, you clear your queue at the end of a sprint, and you can choose a new option at the beginning of your next. 

Even if you have a very complex project and have chosen to stick with the more manual, Flexible transport mode during your implementation in order to have better control, it certainly make sense to switch over to the Simple mode after go live if you only want to focus on the adaption of the newly implemented solution, and only push things to production if there is an update, upgrade from SAP or for some minor fixes, tweaking from the original settings you have made. 

Do remember to re-evaluate this if you are ready for improvement projects, especially when you plan on doing multiple country rollouts, having extension development with complex business process scope activations, Flexible transport mode may still be a better choice.

Check out SAP Activate roadmap for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape) on Transport Management for end-to-end project activity details. 

Stay tuned for more updates and blogs.

Follow the SAP Activate page on the SAP Community. Remember to turn on your notification to receive updates or information about items requiring your attention. To enable notifications, follow the steps on the SAP Community Resources page.

SAP Activate Minute

Tune into the SAP Activate Minute videos in our Playlist here. Subscribe to get notified when we drop new videos. Here the one the Simple Transport Mode

entry.title: S/4HANA - Embedded Analytics: Extensibility Process & Custom CDS View
entry.content:
Overview :

In this blog, we will explore the Extensibility Process and focus on developing custom CDS views using S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. The Extensibility process flow is the key approach used by analytical specialists to develop new Analytical reports and KPIs in the S/4HANA system. SAP presents many standard VDMs to meet the requirements of varying customers and industries. However, there are some specific use cases that demand customized CDS views for their business requirements.

Extensibility Process Flow Diagram:

As depicted in the above diagram, once we identify the standard VDM views from the view browser app, we can create a new custom VDM views to meet the specific requirement of the business. The new custom CDS view, will be used as a source for creating analytical queries. The analytical specialist can create Reports, KPIs and multidimensional reports using these analytical queries.

Step 1:

In S/4HANA Homepage, navigate to “Custom CDS Views App” and proceed  to create a new custom CDS view.

Precondition to create Custom CDS view:

  • Using SAP Released standard CDS views.
  • Using Previously published custom CDS Views

In this CDS view, we have used two standard CDS views and join them using the common key fields. Primary CDS view: overdue payments CDS view and join it with the Secondary CDS view: customer dimension view. We will also create a calculated field to derive ‘high risk’ and ‘low risk’ based on the outstanding amount.

Step 2:

Select the scenario type as Analytical cube and provide the basic details of the Custom CDS view. Custom CDS view name will be prefixed with ‘YY1’*. There is other scenario type also we can select, as shown below.

The Next screen will be presented with 5 sections. We need to maintain all these sections, namely “Data sources”, “Parameters”, “Elements”, “Element Properties”, “Filters”.

As shown above , In the  “Data sources” a section, we will click the “Add” button on the top right corner of the table. Select the primary data sources from the list of released views. Also select the Associate data sources and provide the join condition.

Step 3:

The Next section is “Parameters” section, In Most cases CDS views will produce empty records, if not maintained the parameter values correctly. For example, in the standard cds view (shown below), there are list of parameters maintained, that we need to assign values to fetch the desired results.

In this example we have provided the values for display currency and ExchangeRateType to provide the valid output.

Step 4:

In the “Elements” section, we will select the required attributes of the primary data source fields and associate data source fields. As pointed out below, there are elements, calculations, and parameter options.

Once you have selected the required fields from the “Elements” option, additional calculated fields can be created using the “Calculation” option.

Step 5:

As mentioned in the above step, select the calculation option, and provide a meaning full name for the calculated field. In this case we are trying to find out Credit Risk by considering the outstanding due amount. Hence “Z_CD_CreditRisk” variable uses the “CASE” function. We applied the logic - if overdue amount more than 10000 it will be termed as “High credit risk”.  

Please note the Functions options provides lot of flexibility in applying logics. We can use these calculated fields in our custom cds view reports. The output of this calculated fields is clearly shown in this blog's output section.

Step 6:

In the filter section, we can restrict the output by any selected field available from the report. In this example we have we have restricted overdue amount greater than 3000 euros to be shown in the report.

Step 7:

After Providing required information in these 5 sections, procced to “Check” the report for any issues or syntax errors. This option is available in the bottom of the table section. After the confirmation message, we can procced to publish the report. This will notify us “Publishing successful” as shown below.

The Output of the newly created custom cds view will look like the one below.  You can notice the calculated field “Z_CD_CreditRisk” is featuring in the report.  Based on the overdue amount it updates the risk level in the report.

Conclusion:

This blog provides us with clarity on how to build and use “custom cds views” in a step-by-step manner. This demonstrates the S/4HANA Embedded Analytics capability in a simple-to-use and easy-to-implement way for our SAP customers. Hope our customers will greatly benefit from this embedded analytics capability of SAP S/4HANA for their dynamic business requirements.

Thank You for your time!!!

entry.title: Empowering Public Sector Users of SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Business AI
entry.content:
For many governments around the world, driving efficiency and leveraging technological advances to increase workforce productivity are high on their list of priorities. At SAP, we harness the right AI tools working within the context of your data, to offer you real-world results that make a difference to your business. This is what we refer to as SAP Business AI and SAP Joule is your business AI assistant, grounded in SAP data. 

After the 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, government agencies can look forward to AI innovations (SAP Road Map Explorer) that SAP is planning to introduce next in Public Sector Management (PSM). The PSM scope bundle consists of Grants Management, Budget Management and Funds Accounting. Specifically, SAP will be adding budget and grant management context to SAP Joule. With context awareness, SAP Joule is designed to streamline and improve the user experience, allowing users to efficiently navigate their financial systems and retrieve information to make better strategic decisions. New users typically require extensive training, SAP Joule can quickly provide definitions, summaries, and how-to guidance, saving time and reducing enquiries to IT departments.

Let us have a look at what these planned changes entail.

The User Experience - Reimagined

Interactions with SAP Joule are carried out using natural language, just like how you communicate with your friendly colleagues each day! No learning curve to overcome before you get the answers you need. 

Use SAP Joule to get quick answers to your questions and save time searching through lengthy documentation. No question is too basic for Joule and Joule can even make sense of your shorthand!
  

Efficient Navigation and Task Execution

Today, SAP supports many customers across the globe who run highly complex business processes. Users often utilize different functions and screens to perform multiple transactions. SAP Joule can help users quickly and efficiently navigate to relevant applications to complete their tasks. In our Early Adopter program, participants have reported time savings of up to 90% when performing navigational and transactional tasks. With Joule’s help, a task that used to require 5 minutes was reduced to 30 seconds. This can potentially lead to significant cost savings for public sector organizations. Let’s estimate the potential savings an agency with 10,000 employees could realize: 

 

Joule can help reduce the time to perform routine tasks by quickly bringing you to the relevant application screen. Instead of navigating through menu options, simply enter a short query such as “Show Funds”. You will be provided with options to access the relevant screen directly or to read related documentation. 

Click on the “Open” button and SAP Joule directly brings you to the “Manage Funds” screen where you can search and display information about various funds. You can create or delete funds, or access individual funds to make changes.

A new user can quickly learn about the “Manage Funds” application by clicking on the SAP Help link provided by Joule.

Here’s another example of how you can easily navigate the application and perform your tasks more quickly with the help of SAP Joule:

To make changes to a grant, simply say what you want to do and SAP Joule will guide you.

When you click on the “Open” button to manage grants, SAP Joule brings you directly to the screen where you can view or amend details of the grant.

A user who is unfamiliar with Grants Management can learn more by clicking on the SAP Help link provided by Joule. Joule ensures that the most relevant content is presented to you right from the start, saving you time scrolling through multiple pages of less important information.


We are just starting our journey with SAP Joule, and our customers can expect more AI innovations to be added across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Information on these plans can be found on the SAP Road Map Explorer. To find out more about AI and its potential to serve your needs in the public sector, please read our whitepaper, “AI in the Public Sector”.

entry.title: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
In this blog post I’d like to outline the innovations for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 in the area of Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management. These are the topics that are covered:

  • Reserving Material Using Project Demand (4RF)
  • Creating a Project by Copying an Existing Project Using an API (1NT, 1YF)
  • Integration with Project Plan Collaboration (6JN)

Check out my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

 

Reserving Material Using Project Demand

To simplify project planning, we’ve built on our functionality for project demands. A project demand represents a certain need of a particular WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) element at a specific time and location. It enables you to plan, trigger and monitor your logistics in a single application within your enterprise projects.

Meanwhile, we offer 3 demand categories: Material, service and resource demands with different demand types to cover different business scenarios. In case you’re interested to learn more about project demands, please have a look at my dedicated blog post.

As part of the material demands, we introduce the new demand type “Project Stock, Reservation”. With this type, a project procurement controller has the option to reserve a certain quantity of a material for the project at a certain date. The respective material is reserved from project stock.

When the demand gets requested, the reservation item gets generated. With this release, we do have the restriction that the reservations which are generated out of the project do not generate requirements. However, with the upcoming releases we plan on having the functionality in place so that an MRP run reads the reservation and automatically triggers the requirements if needed.

Figure 1: The new demand type “Project Stock, Reservation” enables project procurement controllers to plan and reserve material for a project from project stock

 

 

Creating a Project by Copying an Existing Project Using an API

This highlight can be extremely time saving when creating projects. We have enhanced the already existing API “Enterprise Project” which you can make use of for enterprise projects as well as professional services projects. In addition to the functionality which was available even before this release, which is create, read, update and delete the project definition or the project elements of projects, we now also allow for the copying of projects.

When copying a project with this API, the API takes an existing project as reference and automatically creates a new project by copying this reference project. Along with the project, all parameters are copied as well such as the appropriate IDS, planned dates, the demands etc. Moreover, you have the possibility to copy settlement rules.

Figure 2: The enhanced “Enterprise Project” API allows for automatic creation of a project based on a reference project.

 

 

Integration with Project Plan Collaboration

With SAP Project and Resource Management (formerly known as SAP S/4HANA Cloud for projects) we offer two services: project management and resource management.

With Project Management as part of SAP Project and Resource Management, you can manage the project from an execution point of view. What we now offer are public APIs to create project plans and allow for interaction from outside with the project plan functionality in SAP Project and Resource Management. Having this feature in place means that the integration in both directions is possible and you can easily access external tools to consolidate project information. As asynchronous processing is allowed, process efficiency is increased.

Moreover, we offer now as well direct integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by direct API calls. With this we reduce TCO for our customers because the SAP Integration Suite is not a prerequisite anymore. Importing project plans can happen for enterprise projects as well as professional services projects. The user can create related object entries to allow for navigation between the user interfaces. On top of the initial import, we provide update functionality to keep existing project plans consistent in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Project and Resource Management.

Figure 3: The integration with “Project Plan Collaboration” via public APIs allows you to interact from outside with the project plan functionality in SAP Project and Resource Management.

 

 

Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative “SAP Project and Resource Management – Scheduling of Projects”

In case you want to learn more about this bi-directional integration and especially the project scheduling capabilities we offer as part of SAP Project and Resource Management, we highly recommend you to participate in the new Customer Engagement Initiative “

Figure 4: Join the SAP Customer Influence Program for SAP Project and Resource Management - Scheduling of projects.

 

 

More Information

If you are interested in the latest and greatest blog posts on Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management, please have a look at my collection blog and also take a look at the Community page for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management (Project Control) and also check out the blog post on Professional Services published by my colleague Katharina Stopf.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Community to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

 

📽 Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 30-minute-long recording and a presentation for Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

 

🔎Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.content:

Our customers seamlessly connect their billing processes with other critical business functions via the end-to-end suite powered by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Subscription Billing.  

Being named a Leader in Gartner®’s Magic Quadrant™ is a significant achievement, not just for SAP but for our entire ecosystem of customers, partners, and community members.
